title: Who made the only funny joke that evening and why?

meanings.usage: 指使人发笑的戏剧或表演形式
常与up连用，表示排队等候
用于表达令人失望或糟糕的情况
形容缺乏趣味或刺激的事物
指专业表演者，如歌手、舞者等
指为产品或服务做宣传的人
用于强调某事清晰可见或毋庸置疑
描述人因紧张而感到不安的状态
形容动作不自然或不熟练
用于扩大声音的电子设备
fail to do something表示未能做成某事
表示不确定的较小数量
对女性听众或观众的正式称呼

examples.en: When the news got round, everyone came to see.
examples.zh: 消息传开后，大家都来看了。

examples.en: We had to queue for hours to get in.
examples.zh: 我们不得不排了好几个小时的队才能进去。

examples.en: As soon as he opened his mouth, everyone burst out laughing.
examples.zh: 他一开口，大家就突然大笑起来。

examples.en: He stood awkwardly before the microphone.
examples.zh: 他尴尬地站在麦克风前。

examples.en: He spoke at the beginning of the programme.
Those who failed to get in need not have felt disappointed.
examples.zh: 那些没能进去的人不必感到失望。

examples.en: Many of the artistes who should have appeared did not come.
examples.zh: 许多本应该出现的艺人没有来。

examples.en: Those who failed to get in need not have felt disappointed.
examples.zh: 那些没能进去的人本不必感到失望。

examples.en: As soon as he opened his mouth, everyone burst out laughing.
examples.zh: 他一开口，大家就大笑起来。

phrases.usage: 女士们，晚上好（的错误说法）

examples.en: What he actually said was: 'Good ladies, evening and gentlemen!'
examples.zh: 他实际说的是：'女士们好，晚上和先生们！'

grammar.title: 情态动词need的用法（needn't have done）
grammar.definition: needn't have + 过去分词表示本不必做某事（但实际上已经做了）
grammar.structure: 主语 + needn't have + 过去分词
grammar.usage: 用于表达某动作是不必要的，但已经发生了

examples.en: Those who failed to get in need not have felt disappointed.
examples.zh: 那些没能进去的人本不必感到失望。

examples.en: You needn't have waited for me.

grammar.title: 情态动词should的用法（should have done）
grammar.definition: should have + 过去分词表示本应该做某事（但实际没做）
grammar.structure: 主语 + should have + 过去分词
grammar.usage: 用于表达遗憾或责备，暗示期望与现实的差距

examples.en: Many of the artistes who should have appeared did not come.
examples.zh: 许多本应该出现的艺人没有来。

examples.en: I should have studied harder for the exam.
examples.zh: 我本应该更努力学习考试的。

grammar.title: when引导的时间状语从句
grammar.definition: when引导的时间状语从句，说明主句动作发生的背景或时机
grammar.usage: when在此处意为'当...的时候'或'当...的消息传开时'

examples.en: When the news got round that a comedy show would be presented, we all rushed to see it.
examples.zh: 当免费喜剧表演的消息传开时，我们都赶去看。

sentencePatterns.pattern: People will do anything to + 动词原形

original.en: People will do anything to see a free show--even if it is a bad one.
original.zh: 人们会做任何事去看免费表演——即使是很糟糕的。

imitations.en: People will do anything to get a bargain.
imitations.zh: 人们会做任何事来买到便宜货。

imitations.en: Students will do anything to pass the exam.
imitations.zh: 学生们会做任何事来通过考试。

sentencePatterns.pattern: When the news got round that...

original.en: When the news got round that a comedy show would be presented at our local cinema, we all rushed to see it.
original.zh: 当喜剧表演将在当地电影院上演的消息传开时，我们都赶去看。

imitations.en: When the news got round that the store was closing down, everyone rushed there.
imitations.zh: 当那家店即将关门的消息传开时，大家都赶了过去。

imitations.en: When the news got round that he won the lottery, his phone never stopped ringing.
imitations.zh: 当他中彩票的消息传开时，他的电话响个不停。

original.en: The only funny things we heard that evening came from the advertiser.
original.zh: 那天晚上我们听到的唯一有趣的事情来自那个广告商。

imitations.en: The only food left in the fridge was some old bread.
imitations.zh: 冰箱里剩下的唯一食物是一些旧面包。

imitations.en: The only person who understood the problem was the teacher.
imitations.zh: 唯一理解这个问题的人是老师。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + be obviously + 形容词/副词

original.en: He was obviously very nervous and for some minutes stood awkwardly before the microphone.
original.zh: 他显然非常紧张，在麦克风前笨拙地站了好几分钟。

imitations.en: She was obviously very tired after the long journey.
imitations.zh: 长途旅行后，她显然非常疲惫。

imitations.en: The student was obviously very nervous during the presentation.
imitations.zh: 那个学生在做报告时显然非常紧张。
